methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals intentional distribution of AI literacy beyond elite institutions, creating peer learning networks that could democratize capability across Richmond's workforce rather than concentrating it in corporate tech hubs.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI pressure on Richmond's young workforce indicates the metro is experiencing technology displacement effects common to concentrated innovation zones, where shallow platform adoption disrupts jobs without building distributed deep capability to respond.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents bottom-up capability building in a historically underserved region, potentially creating distributed skill development that doesn't rely on NoVA's tech infrastructure to trickle down.

signals.title: Simplilearn Partners With Virginia Tech to Launch Applied Generative AI Specialization - PR Newswire UK
signals.signal_reading: Virginia Tech's Applied Generative AI Specialization with Simplilearn reinforces the New River Valley as a deep capability pocket, but partnership with an online platform could enable distribution if learners across Virginia access it beyond Blacksburg.

signals.title: Virginia Police AI Savings: $3M - StartupHub.ai
signals.signal_reading: Police AI generating $3M in savings suggests shallow, platform-delivered efficiency gains distributed across departments statewide—cost reduction without building internal AI development capacity or deep technical understanding in public sector workers.
